pm_research_plan: Problem: Establishing a tuition center in Penang requires clarity on which customer segments to serve, what learning outcomes or formats they will pay for, how location and facilities influence enrollment and pricing, and whether unit economics, regulations, and competitive dynamics support a sustainable business. Target Users: Primary: Parents of school-age children in Penang seeking academic improvement or enrichment. Secondary: Adult learners or professionals needing upskilling, test preparation, or flexible schedules. Tertiary: Corporate or institutional clients needing group training or customized programs. MVP Goal: Define a minimum viable tuition center concept (subjects, grades, format, pricing, and facility level) that can validate demand and basic unit economics in Penang within a constrained pilot period. Success Metrics: - Number of paying students within first 3–6 months - S ... [compressed 3009 chars] ... s are required to operate legally? - MVP Scope Guard: What is the smallest set of subjects, grades, and services needed to test demand without overbuilding facilities or staff? Decision Criteria: - Prioritize options that minimize upfront investment while maximizing learning signal from real customers. - Favor segments and subjects with clear, measurable demand and manageable competition. - Location and facility choices must balance cost, accessibility, and customer expectations. - Regulatory feasibility is non-negotiable; any concept that cannot be licensed or operated safely is out of scope.
